purpose: Canonical, human-authored metadata that maps engineering tasks/milestones to owner-facing PRODUCT SYSTEMS and assigns explicit weights, so the read-only owner dashboard can compute two deterministic progress numbers and render an owner-friendly Product Map. This file is the ONLY new source of truth the dashboard adds; it references task/milestone ids from project-control but never restates their live status (status always comes from the ledger). Weights and plain-English text are editorial and owner-tunable; the validator (tools/validate_product_map.py) enforces that every ledger task maps to exactly one system, that eng_weight and launch_weight each sum to 100, and that every referenced id resolves.

progress_model.engineering_completion: Sum over systems of eng_weight * completion_fraction, where completion_fraction = (sum of progress_percent over the system's contracted ledger tasks) / (100 * expected_count), expected_count = max(contracted_count, planned_count), clamped to [0,1]. This CREDITS built-but-unaccepted code (e.g. M4 rules merged as draft at ~98%) and counts planned-but-uncontracted scope (M3/M5) as incomplete rather than 100%-of-zero. Deterministic; recomputable from ledger + these weights; never AI-estimated.
progress_model.launch_readiness: Sum over systems of launch_weight * readiness_fraction, where readiness_fraction = (count of ACCEPTED ledger tasks in the system) / max(contracted_count, planned_count), then reduced by any readiness_cap that still applies (e.g. rules_engine is capped until the G6 qualified-human legal gate passes; auth is 0 while its tasks are blocked). This measures what a real architect can SAFELY rely on today — verified/accepted capability, not merged draft code. Deterministic; deliberately lower than and divergent from engineering_completion.
progress_model.notes: If the ledger or this file cannot be parsed/validated, the dashboard must show 'Progress unavailable - project state could not be verified' and never a fabricated number.

systems.name: Cloud Foundation & Control Plane
systems.owner_purpose: The engineering backbone: the monorepo, automated testing (CI), the contracts every part agrees on, and the deploy/rollback machinery.
systems.owner_why: Nothing else can be built or shipped safely without it. It is the ground the whole product stands on.

systems.name: Accounts, Security & Production Infrastructure
systems.owner_purpose: Sign-in, separate organizations/accounts, row-level data security, audit logging, and the production database project.
systems.owner_why: A real multi-user architect beta cannot ship without real accounts and strict data isolation. Currently blocked on a cloud credential.

systems.name: Official NYC Data Connectors
systems.owner_purpose: Versioned, provenance-tracked connections to official NYC sources (PLUTO, zoning GIS, tax-lot geometry, etc.) with health checks and contract tests.
systems.owner_why: Every fact the product states about a property must come from an official source, with a traceable record of where it came from.

systems.name: Property Intelligence (Profile & Confirm)
systems.owner_purpose: Builds one canonical property profile from official data - address, BBL, lot area/geometry, existing building, zoning districts/overlays/special districts, and any data conflicts - and lets the user confirm it.
systems.owner_why: This is the trustworthy input to everything downstream. If the profile is wrong, every later answer is wrong.

systems.name: Survey & Document Ingestion
systems.owner_purpose: Ingest surveys and official documents, extract and verify their facts, and offer a review UI.
systems.owner_why: Improves accuracy where public data is thin - but it is on hold by owner decision and is not required for the first architect beta.

systems.name: Versioned Legal Corpus
systems.owner_purpose: The full Zoning Resolution and related codes, ingested with section hierarchy, tables, cross-references, effective-date versions, and citation anchors.
systems.owner_why: Rules cannot be legally published or verified without the versioned source text they cite. This is a prerequisite for trustworthy zoning answers and has not been started.

systems.name: Zoning Rules Engine & Professional Review
systems.owner_purpose: A deterministic engine that decides which zoning rules apply and calculates what they permit, with a review workflow so a qualified professional can approve each rule.
systems.owner_why: This is the heart of the product - turning zoning law into reliable buildable numbers. The engine is built in draft, but NO rule is legally verified yet (it awaits qualified-human sign-off, gate G6).

readiness_cap.reason: Rules stay draft/needs-review until a qualified human legally approves them (G6). Until then an architect cannot rely on the numbers, no matter how much engineering is complete.

systems.name: Scenario & Optimization Engine
systems.owner_purpose: Generates several materially different development options that respect the rules, then scores and ranks them by the owner's chosen objectives.
systems.owner_why: This is the value an architect actually evaluates - a set of ranked, traceable what-you-could-build options. Not started yet.

systems.name: Reporting, Validation & Production Launch
systems.owner_purpose: Reproducible PDF/Excel/JSON reports with full provenance, golden-property validation against manual analysis, plus the monitoring and hardening needed to run in production.
systems.owner_why: An architect needs a defensible, exportable report, proven to match expert analysis. Not started yet.

systems.name: 3D Massing & Advanced Capabilities
systems.owner_purpose: Interactive 3D massing review, plus advanced production capabilities and Revit integration.
systems.owner_why: Valuable later, but explicitly out of scope for the first architect beta and partly under an owner hold.

M0-T007.owner_description: Stand up the real cloud database (Supabase) with migrations. Blocked waiting on an owner-provided access credential.

M0-T008.owner_description: Sign-in, per-organization accounts, row-level security, and audit logging so each customer only sees their own data.

M0-T019.owner_description: Lock down the web app so browser clients cannot reach internal-only capabilities.

M0-T021.owner_description: Prove the exact software versions used in production are the reviewed, hash-pinned ones.

M2-T012.owner_description: Combine all official facts into one clean, confirmable property profile.

M2-T013.owner_description: Work out which zoning districts and overlays actually touch the lot, including split-lot cases, with honest uncertainty ranges.

M2-T014.owner_description: Plan how to ingest surveys/official documents. On hold by owner decision.

M2-T015.owner_description: Plan how to extract and verify facts from ingested documents. On hold.

M2-T016.owner_description: Plan the reviewer screen for ingested documents. On hold.

M4-T001.owner_description: The deterministic engine and the first family of residential floor-area rules, built in DRAFT. Awaiting a qualified human's legal sign-off before any rule is trusted.

M4-T002.owner_description: Feed verified property + zoning facts into the rules engine so it can begin evaluating what a lot is allowed to build.

M4-T003.owner_description: Safety and correctness hardening for the rules engine's inputs and edge cases.

M4-T004.owner_description: Additional fail-closed safeguards so impossible inputs never produce a misleading legal result.

M4-T005.owner_description: An internal-only endpoint and screen that surface a draft rule-evaluation result, clearly marked not-verified, without exposing it in production.

M6-T001.owner_description: The beginning of the reproducible, fully-cited architect report.

M0-T022.owner_description: This dashboard: a read-only window into the whole project's real state, in plain English.

blocker_labels.B-001: Cloud database access credential needed from the owner (Supabase).
blocker_labels.B-004: NYC Geoclient API key needed from the owner.
blocker_labels.B-010: A client-provided benchmark sheet is needed to validate rules against real expert analysis.
